keyRules: Maximum of 120 m above ground level (EASA Open Category)
Registration as a UAS operator (free, valid for 3 years) if the drone weighs 250 g or more, or has a camera
Mandatory A1/A3 online training for drones weighing 250 g or more — minimum pilot age: 16 years.
Mandatory liability insurance for drones weighing more than 900 g
Flying over military installations, prisons, embassies, and security forces is prohibited (Decree-Law No. 87/2021). The Sintra-Cascais Natural Park requires authorization from the ICNF.
Always keep the drone within direct line of sight (VLOS)
Never fly over people, crowds, or public events
Do not fly in airport and heliport protection zones (CTR/ATZ) without authorization—check the zones on the app’s map and at voanaboa.pt.
Night flights are permitted in the Open Category without ANAC authorization—turn on a flashing green light on the drone.
Azores and Madeira: Additional regional restrictions may apply
Aerial photography and filming (and the distribution of such material) require authorization from the AAN—applications can be submitted free of charge online via the e-AAN platform.
importantNotes: Portugal has a dual system: ANAC regulates flights, while AAN authorizes the capture of aerial imagery.
Registration is free and valid in all EU/EASA countries
Mandatory insurance for drones weighing more than 900 g
Flying over military installations, prisons, and embassies is prohibited; flights in areas under military jurisdiction require authorization from the AAN.
Wildfire Season: Additional Restrictions May Apply to the Use of Drones
The GDPR applies to the collection of identifiable images

fields.disclaimerText: Always check the official sources from ANAC and AAN before flying. This app is a tool—not a legal authority. Portugal follows EASA regulations with national amendments.
